mission.title: Install and Configure Volcano on Kubernetes
mission.description: Volcano is a Kubernetes-native batch scheduling system that enhances the capabilities of the standard kube-scheduler. It is designed to manage and optimize various batch and elastic workloads, making it ideal for AI, machine learning, and big data applications.

steps.title: Add the Volcano Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Volcano Helm repository to your local Helm configuration. This allows you to install Volcano using Helm.
```bash
helm repo add volcano https://volcano.sh/volcano
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Volcano using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install Volcano with Helm, specifying the namespace and creating it if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install volcano volcano/volcano --namespace volcano --create-namespace --version v1.14.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Volcano pods are running properly in the 'volcano' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace volcano
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Volcano scheduler to optimize performance. Edit the configuration as needed.
```bash
kubectl edit configmap volcano-config --namespace volcano
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Volcano
steps.description:
Ensure that the necessary Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) permissions are set up for Volcano.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/volcano-sh/volcano/master/docs/rbac/volcano-rbac.yaml --namespace volcano
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Volcano release using Helm to remove the deployed resources.
```bash
helm uninstall volcano --namespace volcano
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs
uninstall.description:
Remove the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were installed with Volcano.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep volcano.sh | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the namespace used for Volcano to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace volcano
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of Volcano.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace volcano -o yaml > volcano-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure that your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Volcano
upgrade.description:
Upgrade your existing Volcano installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade volcano volcano/volcano --namespace volcano --version v1.14.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Volcano pods are running the new version properly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace volcano
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability and scheduling issues.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace volcano
```

troubleshooting.title: Volcano scheduler not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Volcano scheduler is not starting, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <scheduler-pod-name> --namespace volcano
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource quota exceeded
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource quota issues, check the resource limits set for the namespace.
```bash
kubectl get resourcequota --namespace volcano
```

troubleshooting.title: Configuration errors
troubleshooting.description:
If there are configuration errors, validate the Volcano configuration map.
```bash
kubectl get configmap volcano-config --namespace volcano -o yaml
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Volcano will have all pods running in the 'volcano' namespace without errors. You can submit batch jobs and monitor their execution through the Volcano scheduler.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add volcano https://volcano.sh/volcano
helm repo update
helm install volcano volcano/volcano --namespace volcano --create-namespace --version v1.14.1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
